response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack engineer, but their profile fundamentally mismatches this role's hard requirements. The position demands native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX development, Linux kernel source-code modification experience, and embedded networking-security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching). The resume shows none of these: languages listed are English, Romanian, and German; C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015; and career focus has been web platforms, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang services rather than embedded firmware or network-switch development.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and open-source contributions
Comfortable on Linux with systems-level work (low-latency DLang services, audio/hardware projects)
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed — native-level Japanese is a hard requirement
No demonstrated Linux kernel modification, networking-security (MACsec, 802.1X), or LAN-switch/embedded firmware experience
C programming experience is minimal and dated; recent decade is almost entirely web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang)

key_factors.factor: No embedded C / Linux kernel / network-security experience
Solid general seniority and engineering craftsmanship
